name: sha256_body_hash
description: SHA-256 of representative request-body payloads, hex-encoded lowercase. These are the values that feed `body_hash_hex` into buildCanonicalPayload (see canonical_request_string.json) and commit to the request body via the canonical signing chain.

producer.tool: node:crypto.createHash('sha256') — used by the reference Core/Brain for body hashing before signing.

notes: Hash output is lowercase hex (no '0x' prefix, no separators, no line breaks) — 64 characters for a 32-byte SHA-256 digest.
Body bytes are UTF-8 encoded before hashing. Strings here are UTF-8; callers with byte arrays should skip encoding.
empty_body is the canonical all-request fixture used whenever a request has no payload. Implementations MUST NOT substitute the empty string with a '0x00'-filled 32-byte hash or any other sentinel.
Unicode case verifies UTF-8 (not UTF-16 / Latin-1) encoding. café résumé encodes to 14 bytes: 63 61 66 c3 a9 20 72 c3 a9 73 75 6d c3 a9.
